no_gaps_directive.primitive: Fuse the residual rank-2 axis-1 I64 AddOne vmap transpose and scalar add in the existing safe-Rust BatchTrace path.
alien_graveyard_lineage: /data/projects/alien_cs_graveyard/alien_cs_graveyard.md: cache-local loop interchange and fused data-parallel primitive application.

profile_backed_target.bead_source: No ready [perf] bead was available outside the peer-owned fj-core/fj-lax lane, so frankenjax-j5j7 was filed from the fresh fj-dispatch Criterion profile.
profile_backed_target.fresh_baseline_command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- dispatch_latency
profile_backed_target.target_gap: The axis-1 rank-2 AddOne path was 31.77x slower than the same rank-2 AddOne axis-0 row and 32.72x slower than vector AddOne, showing transpose/loop overhead after pass29.

optimization.lever: In batch_binary_elementwise, before moving batch_dim=1 to front and then evaluating Add generically, directly emit the transposed rank-2 I64 tensor with the scalar Add already applied.
optimization.guard: Primitive::Add, batch_dim == 1, batched value is rank-2 DType::I64 tensor, scalar value is Literal::I64, and every stored tensor literal is Literal::I64.
optimization.fallback: All non-matching primitive, dtype, rank, axis, non-scalar, and malformed-literal cases return None and use the existing move_batch_dim_to_front plus eval_primitive path.

after.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- dispatch_latency/vmap/rank2_add_one_in_axes_1

benchmark.worker_caveat: Baseline and after were both remote RCH Criterion runs but landed on different workers. The target delta is large enough to clear the score threshold despite worker variance.

isomorphism_proof.ordering_preserved: Yes. The fused loop emits output[col, row] from source[row, col], exactly matching move_batch_dim_to_front for rank-2 axis-1 row-major tensors.
isomorphism_proof.arithmetic: Literal::I64(value.wrapping_add(scalar)) preserves the existing I64 Add semantics for this path. Add with an I64 scalar is commutative under wrapping addition, so scalar-left and scalar-right arms remain equivalent.
isomorphism_proof.shape_and_dtype: Yes. Output shape is [cols, rows] and dtype remains DType::I64, matching the old batch-dim move-to-front result.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: N/A. Add has no comparisons or ties.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: N/A for the optimized guard. Floating-point paths are excluded by the DType::I64 and Literal::I64 guards.
isomorphism_proof.rng: N/A. The path consumes no random state.
isomorphism_proof.error_behavior: Preserved by fallback for all mismatched and malformed cases.

validation.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- dispatch_latency/vmap/rank2_add_one_in_axes_1
rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-dispatch --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings

validation.result: exit 0; no criticals; existing warning/info inventory remains
